commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates Android onboarding QR scan failures to show camera-permission-specific recovery copy, request CAMERA after a denied scanner startup failure, offer app settings, and add focused unit coverage.

PR surface: Other +97. Total +97 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level. Current main maps QR scanner startup failure to generic setup copy while camera permission is modeled only in the later permission step; the linked issue and contributor proof show the denied-camera setup path.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair is appropriate because the remaining blocker is a concrete permission-state synchronization bug in the touched Android onboarding files.

target.title: fix(android): explain camera permission for setup scans
target.body:
Closes #98044.

### What Problem This Solves
When Android setup QR scanning failed because Camera permission was denied, onboarding only showed generic scanner failure copy and did not guide users to grant the permission.

### Evidence
At PR head `95764eea4c`, focused Play and third-party unit tests pass for `OnboardingFlowLogicTest`. They cover camera-specific scanner copy, the app-settings recovery decision, and that non-camera setup errors do not show the camera settings action.

Android emulator proof on `sdk_gphone64_arm64`, Android 16 / API 36, using the Play debug APK (`versionName=2026.6.10`, `targetSdk=36`): with Camera revoked for OpenClaw and Google Play services, `Connect Gateway` -> `Scan setup code` first showed Google scanner `Camera permission required`; after dismissing it, Android requested `Allow OpenClaw Node to take pictures and record video?`; after `Don’t allow`, Gateway Setup showed `Camera permission is required to scan setup codes. Open Android app settings to grant Camera permission, then try again.` with the `Open app settings` action. A follow-up UI dump after leaving the camera-specific state no longer contained `Open app settings`.
target.author: turbotheturtle

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
